source_jobs.title: Implementation Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Molecule Software | Poland (Office)

Molecule is a modern ETRM platform built for energy trading companies across power, natural gas, renewables, and financial commodities. We're looking for an Implementation Engineer to join our Poland-based technical team and take ownership of the build work at the core of every client implementation.




THE ROLE

Working from requirements and solution plans produced by Implementation BAs and Solution Architects, you'll configure Molecule environments, load and validate data, and execute the testing that takes a client from signed design to live system. You'll also carry support responsibilities for live clients, investigating and resolving technical issues across configuration, data, and platform behaviour.

This is a back-office, internally-facing role — you'll work closely with BAs, Solution Architects, and the Technical Team Manager in a structured, documentation-driven delivery environment.




WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON

• Configuring Molecule across complex instrument types: power (OTC, PPAs, GoOs, exchange-traded), natural gas (physical and financial), financial instruments (FTRs, RECs, RGGIs, options, swaps), and structured contracts with tiered or ratcheted pricing

• Loading and validating reference data, historical trades, market pricing, and settlement data

• Executing reconciliations and unit testing before UAT; supporting BAs through client review

• Investigating and resolving support tickets covering configuration errors, data issues, valuation discrepancies, and settlement anomalies

• BigBang data migration and validation




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

• Background in energy, commodities, or financial services — a working understanding of physical and financial trading, trade lifecycles, and post-trade processes is required

• Hands-on experience with an ETRM or CTRM platform is essential

• Strong SQL: querying, validating, and transforming trade, position, market data, and reference data

• Familiarity with energy market concepts: forward curves, MTM valuation, P&L attribution, settlement

• Experience working from structured specification documents with a clear instinct to raise blockers early

• Strong written English for documentation and async communication

source_jobs.title: Technical Team Manager
source_jobs.description:
Molecule Software | Poland (Office)

Molecule is a modern ETRM platform serving energy trading companies across power, gas, renewables, and financial commodities. As we scale our implementation business, we're hiring a Technical Team Manager to lead our Poland-based technical (build and support) team.




THE ROLE

You'll lead a team of Implementation Engineers responsible for the technical configuration, data loading, testing, and support work that underpins every Molecule implementation. This is a player-manager position — you'll carry a hands-on delivery contribution alongside managing the team's workload, standards, and development.

You sit between Implementation BAs (who own client relationships and requirements) and the build team (who execute against them). Your job is to make sure work is clearly understood, accurately executed, and delivered on time — and to build the processes that make that repeatable across a growing portfolio of concurrent implementations.




WHAT YOU'LL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR

• Managing, mentoring, and developing a team of Implementation Engineers

• Owning quality and timeliness of all build team outputs: ETRM configuration, data loading, testing, reconciliation, and support

• Defining and maintaining the operating model between BAs and the technical team

• Resource allocation across concurrent implementations and support queues

• Driving continuous improvement — turning recurring issues into structural fixes

• Reporting on team throughput and implementation status to CS leadership




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

• Hands-on background in energy, commodities, or financial services — physical and/or financial trading, post-trade processes, ETRM/CTRM experience required

• Prior experience managing a technical delivery or implementation team in a SaaS or enterprise software environment

• Strong SQL skills and the ability to independently validate trade, position, and market data outputs

• Strong process orientation — comfortable building structure where ways of working are still maturing

• Excellent written and spoken English; experience with distributed or offshore teams is a strong advantage

source_jobs.title: Integration Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Molecule Software | Poland (Office)

Molecule is a modern ETRM platform built for energy trading companies across power, natural gas, renewables, and financial commodities. We're growing our integration capability and looking for an Integration Engineer to own the technical connectivity that sits at the heart of every client implementation.




THE ROLE

You'll design, build, and maintain the integrations that connect Molecule to the systems our clients depend on — market data providers, exchange platforms, generation data sources, ERP and CRM systems, SCADA historians, and downstream analytics infrastructure. You'll also build custom reports in SQL, turning client data into the insights they need.

This is an end-to-end ownership role: from integration design through to testing, documentation, and handover. Where your profile supports it, you'll engage directly with client technical teams — representing Molecule in integration design discussions and supporting go-live.




WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON

• Building integrations with ISO and market data providers (Montel, Pharos, ZEMA), exchange platforms (ICE, CME, Nodal), ERP/CRM systems (SAP, Microsoft), and generation data sources

• API-based price extraction — forward curves, LMPs, ancillary service prices, settlement data

• Outbound data feeds and BigBang data lake integration for positions, P&L, and valuations

• REST APIs, SFTP/FTP, webhooks, and email-based automation

• SSO and MFA configuration

• Custom report development in Mode using SQL




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

• Background in energy, commodities, or financial services — familiarity with ETRM/CTRM workflows is strongly preferred

• Strong Python and TypeScript — both used daily

• Solid SQL for data transformation, validation, and reconciliation

• Hands-on experience with real-world integrations: market data vendors, exchanges, ERP/CRM, or SCADA systems

• Good understanding of REST APIs, OAuth 2.0, SAML, and common data formats (JSON, XML, CSV, FIX)

notes: Small team, high tech role ratio, likely startup
